MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-06-2014 Continued To Sep-08-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Defendant Mazzetti Nash Lipsey Burch Inc.'S Motion For Summary Judgment / Adjudication
Off Calendar Jun-04-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Venardi, Esq., 925.937.3900, for plaintiff Yefim Sheynis.
Rachel Moroski, Esq. and Mark Posard, Esq., 415.986.5900, for defendant Mazzetti Nash Lipsey Burch Inc.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling re: Defendant Mazzetti Nash Lipsey Burch Inc.'S Motion For Summary Judgment / Adjudication. The motion is granted in part and denied in part. Summary judgment is denied because disputed issues of material fact exist as to at least one cause of action. Summary adjudication is granted in favor of Defendant as to Plaintiff's claims for harassment and retaliation. Demotions are specifically listed as actions that do not come within the meaning of harassment, and are not actionable under a harassment cause of action. (Janken v. GM Hughes Electronics (1996) 46 Cal.App.4th 55, 63.) Moreover, the allegedly harassing statements fall short of the required showing of a "pattern of continuous, pervasive, harassment." (Mokler v. County of Orange (2007) 157 Cal.App.4th 121, 141-42.) As to the retaliation claim, the adverse action occurred nearly a year after Plaintiff allegedly made the complaint, and there is no other evidence to support the contention that Plaintiff's email to Karmiris motivated his termination. However, summary adjudication is denied as to Plaintiff's claims for age discrimination, failure to prevent discrimination, and wrongful termination. Plaintiff has established that he suffered adverse action when he was demoted and terminated, and that there is a triable issue of material fact as to the presence of a discriminatory motive and whether Defendant's proffered explanation was pretext for discrimination. (Def.'s Resp. to Pl.'s Separate Statement in Supp. Of Mot. For Summ. J. # 10, 18, 20, and 24.) Moreover, there are triable issues of material fact as to whether Plaintiff's email to Karmiris constituted a sufficient complaint of discrimination, and whether Vernon had a duty to either investigate or inform another employee of the complaint, therefore foreclosing summary adjudication on the failure to prevent discrimination claim. (Id. # 36, 41.) Finally, Defendant relies solely on the failure of Plaintiff's other claims to support summary adjudication of the wrongful termination claim. Thus, because Plaintiff has a viable claim for age discrimination, summary adjudication on this cause of action is denied. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication
Off Calendar Jun-03-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopted the tentative ruling. DEFENDANT MAZZETTI NASH LIPSEY BURCH INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Adjudication is continued to June 4, 2014 at 9:30 am in Dept. 302 per the agreement of the parties. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.
